title: Podman on Windows with WSL2 throw Error: no container with name or ID xxxxxx found: no such container
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Podman on Windows with WSL2 throw Error: no container with name or ID xxxxxx found: no such container

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create docker-compose-mysql.yml file
```
version: '3'

services:

 mysql:
   image: mysql:8.0.23
   restart: always
   ports:
     - "3306:3306"
   volumes:
     - '/root/mysql/conf:/etc/mysql/conf.d'
     - '/root/mysql/datadir:/var/lib/mysql'
   environment:
     MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: 123456
```

2.  run podman-compose up 
     `podman-compose -f docker-compose-mysql.yml up`


**Describe the results you received:**

```
using podman version: podman version 3.0.1
podman pod create --name=root --share net -p 3306:3306
Error: unable to create pod cgroup for pod e08d69e665de8bbf0ec486d9ce4489e9c349bcd017b10f05a6a2d9b6ed967b29: error creating cgroup machine.slice/machine-libpod_pod_e08d69e665de8bbf0ec486d9ce4489e9c349bcd017b10f05a6a2d9b6ed967b29.slice: dial unix /run/systemd/private: connect: no such file or directory
125
podman create --name=root_mysql_1 --pod=root --label io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 --label io.podman.compose.project=root --label io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 --label com.docker.compose.container-number=1 --label com.docker.compose.service=mysql -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=123456 -v /root/mysql/conf:/etc/mysql/conf.d -v /root/mysql/datadir:/var/lib/mysql --add-host mysql:127.0.0.1 --add-host root_mysql_1:127.0.0.1 --restart always mysql:8.0.23
Error: error retrieving pod root: no pod with name or ID root found: no such pod
125
podman start -a root_mysql_1
Error: no container with name or ID root_mysql_1 found: no such container
125
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

 Run successfully

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Linux version 5.4.72-microsoft-standard-WSL2 (oe-user@oe-host) (gcc version 8.2.0 (GCC)) #1 SMP Wed Oct 28 23:40:43 UTC 2020

**Output of `podman version`:**


```
root@DESKTOP-1EFACBD:~# apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 100:3.0.1-2 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 100:3.0.1-2 arm64
podman/unknown 100:3.0.1-2 armhf
podman/unknown 100:3.0.1-2 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Linux version 5.4.72-microsoft-standard-WSL2 (oe-user@oe-host) (gcc version 8.2.0 (GCC)) #1 SMP Wed Oct 28 23:40:43 UTC 2020

comments.body:
`  cgroupManager: systemd`

Here's your problem. You need to edit `containers.conf` to set `cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"`
